description: Trigger discrimination for the Phase 7 AI-platform-ops cluster: AI consumption guardrails vs whole-product cost modeling vs plan gating vs alert implementation vs router logic, and against the shipped saas-cost-architect and observability-operator, plus the D29 compose seams — key custody (secrets-identity-hardener) and error-path fail-closed review (error-handling-security-reviewer).

cases.prompt: Cap token usage per request and per tenant on our LLM endpoint and defend against token-drain abuse.
cases.reason: Per-request/per-tenant AI consumption limits and denial-of-wallet defense are this skill; saas-cost-architect models unit economics and plan-entitlement-architect gates plan features, neither owns the token caps.

cases.prompt: Work out our cost per tenant and per plan tier versus revenue, and where our margins are exposed across compute, storage, and AI.
cases.reason: Whole-product cost drivers, attribution, and unit economics are saas-cost-architect; this skill consumes those numbers to size AI guardrails.

cases.prompt: Define which plan tiers get the AI feature and what monthly usage each tier includes, enforced uniformly across UI and API.
cases.reason: Plan × feature × limit entitlement design is plan-entitlement-architect; this skill composes the plan dimension into runtime consumption caps but doesn't design the entitlement matrix.

cases.prompt: Implement the actual burn-rate alert rules and the cost dashboard for our AI spend in Grafana.
cases.reason: Hands-on alert/dashboard implementation is observability-operator; this skill specifies what to meter and alert on, and hands the spec over.

cases.prompt: Design the model routing and provider fallback logic — which model handles which request and what happens when a provider is down.
cases.reason: Routing/fallback provider logic is ai-router-architect; this skill sets the budget and model-tier intent the router enforces, not the routing implementation.

cases.prompt: Our LLM provider API key got committed to a public repo. Rotate it, move it out of the client bundle into a secret store, and lock down where it's read from.
cases.reason: Key custody, rotation, storage, and client-bundle exposure are secrets-identity-hardener; ai-cost-guardrail-designer composes it and owns only the AI-spend blast radius and per-model invocation restriction of a leaked key, not the custody mechanics.

cases.prompt: Review our catch blocks and fallbacks across the service for any place a failed check defaults to allow instead of deny.
cases.reason: A general error-path fail-closed review across the codebase is error-handling-security-reviewer (OWASP A10); ai-cost-guardrail-designer sets the fail-closed requirement for AI-spend checks specifically but does not review arbitrary error paths.

cases.prompt: If one of our model API keys leaks, how do we make sure it can't be used to run up a huge inference bill on the most expensive model?
cases.reason: Bounding the COST blast radius of a leaked AI key — per-model/least-privilege invocation restriction plus provider-side spend caps — is this skill's AI-spend angle; it composes secrets-identity-hardener for custody/rotation but owns the spend containment, not saas-cost-architect's unit economics.
